specificity.note: Names the exact node (obtainableFactor), the 0.5% band floor and 1% Prospeo anchor, the 83% pre-let / 6.3% vacancy figures, FLAP-D geography, 2023–24 framework signing dates, the cust.operator-large channel, and a concrete perturbation to 0.004. Actors, numbers, instruments and dates are all present.

nonObviousness.note: The join between CRE pre-let statistics (JLL real-estate reports) and PDU orderbook contestability is a genuine cross-document insight, and the whyMissable correctly notes the 1% anchor 'reads as already-haircut'. Docked one point because 'large operators buy via long-lead frameworks' is a known dynamic a founder selling into that channel might independently surface.

mechanismDepth.note: Each hop carries refs (digest:geo-DE#c19, ledger:obtainableFactor, cust.operator-large) and the trigger→propagation→model-impact chain is coherent. The propagation hop — that short-lead rack PDUs ride the same pre-let frameworks as 48–60-month transformers — is the one arrow that would wobble under a hostile 'why?', which the risk's own likelihood rationale concedes.

evidenceQuality.note: The 83%/6.3% trigger has ≥3 independent corroborating sources (JLL, Estates Gazette, Data Centre Review) and lead-time claims are separately sourced. But the load-bearing premise for the perturbation — PDU-specific foreclosure — is only evidenced for transformers/switchgear, a gap the rationale honestly flags via the revisedFrom 0.45→0.42.

decisionRelevance.note: Directly changes Year-1 sequencing via the stated mitigations (re-aim at retrofit/mid-market colo, AVL qualification, project-by-project contestability audit) within the next two quarters. Indicators are monitorable at named venues (TED, JLL half-year prints) with explicit thresholds.
